CROSS DEFENDANT PLM LOAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC., PRIORITY POSTING & PUBLISHING, INC., ANDREW ZHENG Notice Of Joinder Of Defendants Plm Loan Management, Priority Posting, And Andrew Zheng To Plaintiffs Motion To Augment Expert Witness Lis OFF CALENDAR, incorrect entry. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FARHAD ZAGHI VS. CECELIA KENNEDY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Memorandum Of Points And Authorities For Motion To Augment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday February 11, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT CECELIA KENNEDY Notice Of Memorandum Of Points And Authorities For Motion To Augment GRANTED as follows: P shall (1) arrange for Dr. Ness to produce any medical records not previously produced within 10 days of this ruling; (2) produce Dr. Ness for deposition within 20 days of this ruling for deposition on a date designated by Defendants; and Defendants shall designate experts on the subject of mental health and emotional distress, if they so wish. Under CCP 2034.610 exceptional circumstances permit the relief requested in this case. Any prejudice to the defendants is addressed by the conditions described above and failure to disclose a non-retained expert was a result of inadvertent mistake. Additionally, Plaintiff justice requires that Plaintiff should not be penalized for the acts or omissions of her counsel under the circumstances. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL A. DEKELAITA ET AL VS. GAYLE MCKENZIE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday February 11, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT PETER GUALFETTI Notice Of Motion To Dismiss GRANTED. Trailmobile v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 210 Cal.App.3d 1451. Further, no effort on the part of the plaintiff shown to enter into a stipulation contemplated by CCP 583.230. No due diligence is shown in attempts to serve the defendant. Information regarding service location was easily available on-line and through appearing co-defendants. Finally, the Court notes that the facts recited by the Plaintiffs, including age and health condition of the Plaintiff, suggests that the trial in this case may be set within the next several months. This is highly prejudicial to the moving Defendant, the only party who has not been in the case for the past 2 years, unlike everyone else in the action. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday February 11, 2019 line 5. PLAINTIFF MEAGAN MIELKE Notice Of Motion For Order To Ompel Defendants To (1) Respond To Form Interrogatories-Setone; (2)Respond To Request For Production Of Documents- Set One; (3) Comply With Inspection Demand Of Subject Premises; (4) Appear At Deposition; And (5) Pay Monetary Sanctions GRANTED, subject to opposition. rulings.Case Title: KAI MAN LEE ET AL VS. ZONG YAN LIU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday February 11, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT ZONG LUI DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to: (1) Plead in the complaint that one of the grounds enumerated in SFRO 37.9(a) or (b) is Plaintiff's dominant motive; (2) Plead weather (a) sublet/assignment and move-out is a cumulative violation of a rental agreement provision which "preludes an assignment or sublet" or (b) whether sublet/assignment is a separate violation from move-out, i.e. is there are separate provision in the agreement that precludes the Defendant from moving out independent form subletting/assigning; (3) properly fill out paragraph 6(f): if there is no written agreement remove the check mark; if there is a written rental agreement check one or box boxes following the words "because (specify reason)." Demurrer is otherwise overruled. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MILENA MAIONCHI, TRUSTEE OF VS. CHRISTOPHER GARRISON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday February 11, 2019 line 8. DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER GARRISON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the position that Plaintiff was not entitled to proceed with the rent demand immediately after this Court's decision on a de novo review Plaintiff's rent increase notice validity. CCP 1104 (incorrectly cited by Defendant as CCP 1094) and Ng. v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 4 Cal.4th 29 are not on point. = (501/CFH)
